titleStmt.title: Morale and Home Intelligence Reports
Report: Correspondence between V. Bovenschen and E. Bamford, 7 May 1942

div.note: Under the War Cabinet (42) 49th Conclusions of 15th April, 1942, my Minister is to submit to the War. Cabinet monthly an appreciation of the state of public morale, and to take into account in its preparation the views of other Departments primarily concerned with the home front. I should be grateful if you would send us any matter which you wish to be considered for this purpose.

It may be that some regular report bearing on home front questions is already produced in your Department for domestic purposes and, if so, we should appreciate your placing us on its circulation list. We should, of course, undertake not to circulate it further in this Department without your permission and should use it solely for the preparation of our report to the War Cabinet. Any such report, if you can send it, and any views you wish to contribute should reach here not later than the 20th of each month. Will you please have them addressed to Sylvester Gates, who is the Controller of the Home side of this Ministry and responsible for the preparation of the report?
Sir Frederick V. Bovenschen, KBE, CB,
